title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind
summary: Gemma 4's 31B model achieves fourth place on the LM Arena open model leaderboard despite being 2 to 20 times smaller than competing models at comparable quality. The model runs on a single GPU and can be deployed on mobile devices, multi-GPU hospital systems, and Mac machines via LM Studio.
writeup: The talk from Gus Martins and Ian Ballantyne of Google DeepMind presents Gemma 4's 31B model, which reaches fourth place on the LM Arena open model leaderboard while running 2 to 20 times smaller than competing models at comparable quality. The size advantage matters for ownership and deployment: the model runs on a single GPU and extends to mobile devices, multi-GPU hospital systems, and Mac machines via LM Studio. The core point is that open models can deliver competitive quality without the hardware footprint of larger systems, widening where inference can happen.
